title: Portable Context Control Plane — Active IDs Input
description: Schema for the --active-ids CLI input file. Supplies the three active-ID arrays used as optional selector signals: activeSkillIds, activeToolIds, activeMemoryIds. Class B input — if absent, treat all three arrays as empty []; if present but malformed, emit active_ids_missing warning and treat as empty. Unknown active IDs (IDs not found in componentsById) produce active_id_unknown planning warnings per unknown ID — they do NOT produce reference_unknown SelectionDecision records. ID validation against the registry is NOT performed at schema boundary; validation occurs at the core orchestrator boundary before selector fan-out. Canonical owners: docs/06 §2.1 optional selector signals; docs/11 §4.1; docs/12 §3.4; 15-Q2 resolved.
$comment: Pass 4.9C-2B — Batch B. No code. No runtime. No provider/model calls. No docs edited. Canonical sources: docs/06 §2.1, docs/11 §4.1, docs/12 §3.4.

activeSkillIds.title: Active skill IDs
activeSkillIds.description: IDs of skill components currently active in this context. Strings only — no validation against registry at schema boundary. Unknown IDs produce active_id_unknown warnings at the orchestrator; they do not produce reference_unknown SelectionDecision records. Absent from registry does not mean absent from plan — selectors treat unknown active IDs as absent signals, not as errors that halt planning.

activeToolIds.title: Active tool IDs
activeToolIds.description: IDs of tool components currently active at this runtime. Strings only — no registry validation at schema boundary. Unknown IDs produce active_id_unknown warnings per unknown ID. The tool selector also checks runtimeCapabilities for availability; active-ID signals and runtime-capability signals are independent.

activeMemoryIds.title: Active memory IDs
activeMemoryIds.description: IDs of memory components currently active in this context. Strings only — no registry validation at schema boundary. Unknown IDs produce active_id_unknown warnings per unknown ID.
